acceptance_criteria.description: Given a describe_model JSON fixture, build_graph() returns a ConceptGraph with correct node count (one node per measure, dimension level, calc, and hierarchy) and correct edge count for a known fixture. Panics on malformed JSON.

related_measures(graph, measure_name, depth) returns all measures reachable within `depth` hops via any edge type, in ranked order by graph distance. Returns an empty vec (not an error) when the start node is not found.

causal_paths(graph, target_measure) returns ranked Vec<CausalPath> where each path carries the ordered list of edges and nodes from a root column/calc to the target measure, with evidence_type: Structural on every result. At least one path must be returned for any measure that has derivation edges.

suggest_next_questions(graph, context_measures) returns at least one NextQuestion (a measure node adjacent to context_measures but not in context_measures) when the graph contains unexplored neighbors. Returns empty vec when the context already covers all reachable nodes.
All public API types implement serde Serialize/Deserialize. A round-trip (serialize ConceptGraph to JSON, deserialize back) produces a graph with identical node and edge counts.
build_graph() completes in <50 ms for a describe_model JSON with 500 measures and 2000 edges on a developer laptop (M-series Apple Silicon). Measured via criterion bench.
Edge types cover the full Gen-8 vocabulary: aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from, related_to. Each variant is exercised by at least one unit test.
ConceptGraph can be serialized to a stable JSON file and deserialized back without loss (model-graph-diff foundation). Tested via snapshot comparison in unit tests.

ambiguities_resolved.question: The vision names 'mcp-concept-graph' as the PRD component but the requested crate slug is 'mqo-graph-traversal'. Are these the same scope?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: The Gen-8 context narrows scope to the traversal API surface (find related measures, trace causality chains, suggest next question) as stated in the Gen-8 description. The full mcp-concept-graph PRD also includes the NL model extender anchor — that is out of scope here. This crate is the traversal engine; mcp-concept-graph is the broader component PRD.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the unfakeable metric be tests_pass_count or build_graph latency?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: tests_pass_count is load-bearing because correctness of traversal semantics (AC1–AC5) is binary. Latency (AC6) is a SHOULD, not a MUST — it demotes to a criterion bench and does not gate advance/revert decisions.

five_whys_trace.a: Every tool (grounding-eval, hallucination-guard, gap-miner, causal-tracer) currently re-extracts its own slice of the topology ad hoc, duplicating extraction logic and producing inconsistent edge semantics for the same structural relationships.
five_whys_trace.q: Why build a dedicated graph-traversal crate instead of having each Gen-8 tool parse describe_model JSON itself?

five_whys_trace.a: No: the causal-tracer must return evidence_type: Structural on every path, which requires a shared, authoritative definition of what 'derives_from' means. If each tool defines it independently, the structural guarantee is meaningless — two tools can disagree on whether a given path is causal.
five_whys_trace.q: Why does inconsistent edge semantics across tools matter — can't they just be kept in sync manually?

five_whys_trace.a: Enterprise compliance requires that AI-generated analytics explanations be auditable and structurally grounded — not statistical inference. The query audit chain proves what was asked and computed; the concept graph proves why the result is structurally valid. Without the graph, the causal claim is just an assertion.
five_whys_trace.q: Why is the structural guarantee on causal paths load-bearing for the overall Gen-8 vision?

five_whys_trace.a: Because the foundation crate is the unit of correctness: one test suite, one serde contract, one edge-type enum. If mcp-concept-graph (the broader PRD component) is correct, all consumers inherit correctness. Duplication means correctness diverges silently as the model schema evolves.
five_whys_trace.q: Why does structural grounding need to be in a shared lib rather than duplicated with tests in each consumer?

additional.no_external_db: In-memory only for v1; no file-system persistence required by the crate itself.
additional.no_llm_calls: Graph construction and traversal must be fully deterministic; no network I/O in the hot path.

non_goals: No statistical causal inference — all causal paths are structural (derivation-edge traversal only); confidence is path-length-based, not data-driven.
No external graph database (no Neo4j, no RDF triple store, no persistent graph file in v1).
No LLM calls at any point — the graph is built deterministically from JSON; no embeddings, no semantic similarity.
No narrative composition — that is mcp-narrative-builder; this crate returns structured data only.
No NL query parsing — callers supply measure names and context programmatically; NLU is out of scope.
No audit chain functionality — that belongs to mcp-query-audit-chain.
No MCP tool registration — this is a lib crate; mqo-mcp-server wires it as a dependency.
root_motivation: Every Gen-8 downstream tool (grounding-eval, hallucination-guard, gap-miner, causal-tracer) re-extracts its own slice of the AtScale semantic model topology ad hoc, producing inconsistent traversal semantics and preventing any tool from reasoning structurally about why a metric changed. The root need is a single, shared attributed property graph — buildable deterministically from any describe_model JSON, no LLM calls, no external DB — so that graph traversal (find related measures, trace derivation paths, suggest next questions) is done once with consistent edge semantics and every Gen-8 consumer delegates to this crate.
scope: Build an attributed property graph (ConceptGraph) from describe_model JSON in-process with no external DB or LLM calls.
Expose related_measures(graph, start, depth) — find all measures reachable within depth hops.
Expose causal_paths(graph, target) — return ranked derivation paths with evidence_type: Structural.
Expose suggest_next_questions(graph, context) — return unexplored adjacent measure nodes as candidate next questions.
Define the edge-type vocabulary: aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from, related_to.
Serialize/deserialize ConceptGraph and all public types via serde_json.
Provide a deterministic in-memory adjacency-list representation suitable for embedding in mqo-mcp-server.
user_persona: An MCP server crate author (Rust, AtScale mqo-mcp-server) who needs to wire concept-graph traversal into MCP tool responses. The crate is invoked in-process inside the MCP server on every tool call that requires structural reasoning about the semantic model.
